summary.business_type: E-commerce / personalized digital service (custom cartoon-style photo portraits, Stripe checkout, Next.js on Vercel, Supabase backend)
top_findings: Canonical tags on /precios, /order, /galeria, /sobre, /contacto, /faq all incorrectly point to the homepage instead of self, likely suppressing these pages from search results
hreflang tags advertise en/fr/x-default versions that all resolve to the same Spanish-only homepage URL — broken locale signaling with no real English content behind it
Competing/duplicate Product schema nodes on style pages, plus generic Product/Offer schema bleeding onto non-product pages (home, /order, /precios index)
/order checkout page is indexed at sitemap priority 0.9 with no canonical/noindex, and /order?style=* variants are crawlable — real duplicate-content risk on a transactional page
Blog posts are critically thin (one comparison post measured at ~89 words) despite the site's templating proving capable of 500+ word pages elsewhere
LCP fails Core Web Vitals threshold on all 4 tested pages (2.33s-2.94s); /estilos also fails CLS (0.245, 'poor') due to a dynamically-rendered grid card
quick_wins: Fix canonical tags to self-reference each page (hours, high impact)
Remove the broken en/fr hreflang claims until real localized pages exist (hours)
Redirect www.negasva.shop to the apex domain (minutes)
Add llms.txt summarizing the business, pricing, and turnaround for AI crawlers (1-2 hours)
Render all FAQ answers in visible DOM text instead of hiding 9 of 10 inside a collapsed accordion (half day)

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Full SSR/SSG via Next.js — raw HTML contains complete content, no SPA shell
Strong security headers: HSTS preload, full CSP, COOP/CORP, X-Frame-Options DENY
Clean robots.txt and valid 23-URL sitemap with no crawl-blocking conflicts
LCP image correctly uses fetchPriority="high" and is eagerly loaded

findings.title: Canonical tags point to homepage on 6+ pages
findings.description: /precios, /order, /galeria, /sobre, /contacto, /faq all emit a canonical tag pointing to https://negasva.shop instead of their own URL.
findings.recommendation: Fix the metadata defaults so each page self-canonicalizes; audit the Next.js metadata/layout logic likely responsible for the shared default.

findings.title: Broken hreflang claims
findings.description: hreflang en/fr/x-default tags exist on /precios and /estilos but all point to the Spanish root URL — no actual English or French content exists.
findings.recommendation: Remove the en/fr hreflang entries until real localized pages exist, or build the locales.

findings.title: www subdomain not redirected
findings.description: www.negasva.shop returns 200 instead of redirecting to the apex domain.
findings.recommendation: Add a permanent redirect from www to apex at the Vercel/DNS layer.

findings.title: /seguimiento is noindex,nofollow
findings.description: This order-tracking page blocks all link equity unnecessarily.
findings.recommendation: Confirm intent; if accidental, remove the noindex directive.

findings.title: Identical sitemap lastmod timestamps
findings.description: All 23 URLs share the exact same lastmod to the millisecond, including evergreen blog/legal pages — a build-time stamp, not real content tracking.
findings.recommendation: Generate lastmod from actual content modification time, or omit the field.

findings.title: No IndexNow implementation
findings.description: Faster re-crawl signaling unused.
findings.recommendation: Low priority; implement once other critical fixes ship.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Pricing and 48h turnaround are stated clearly and consistently in both prose and schema
Style detail pages (e.g. /estilos/rick-y-morty) run substantial at ~538 words with FAQ and process sections
Visible customer testimonials with verified-purchase badges build some trust signal

findings.title: Blog posts critically thin
findings.description: A comparison post (rick-y-morty-vs-simpsons) renders only ~89 words of body text even with full JS rendering — far below what a comparison post needs to rank or answer the query.
findings.recommendation: Expand all 6 blog posts to 800-1500 words with real comparative substance; prioritize the confirmed-thinnest, highest-intent post first.

findings.title: Likely templated/duplicate style-page content
findings.description: All four style pages share identical hardcoded pricing in schema, suggesting copy-paste-and-swap-name patterning rather than genuinely differentiated content.
findings.recommendation: Differentiate each style page with unique example imagery, unique FAQ answers, and accurate per-style pricing if it varies.

findings.title: Trademark/IP exposure in core keyword strategy
findings.description: Primary keywords and URL slugs are built on third-party copyrighted character names (Rick y Morty, Los Simpsons, Gravity Falls, Padrinos Mágicos/Fairly OddParents).
findings.recommendation: This is a business decision, not purely an SEO one — flagged so it's made deliberately. Consider parody/fan-art framing and a non-trademarked descriptive fallback alongside character names.

findings.title: Testimonials unverifiable, not platform-linked
findings.description: "1000+ clientes felices" has no link-out to a verifiable review platform (Trustpilot, Google Reviews).
findings.recommendation: Link to a real review platform if the claim is genuine; this also unlocks Review/AggregateRating schema.

findings.title: Uneven blog topic coverage
findings.description: 2 of 4 product styles (Gravity Falls, Padrinos Mágicos) have zero supporting blog content; no gift-occasion content despite a gift-driven business model.
findings.recommendation: Add occasion-based content (birthdays, anniversaries) and close the per-style coverage gap.

categories.name: On-Page / Search Experience
what_works: /estilos/rick-y-morty is a well-built hybrid product page: price range, FAQ, breadcrumbs, cross-links — strong fit for gift/commerce-intent searches
/precios server-renders real worked pricing examples in plain HTML, good for both users and citation

findings.title: Page-type mismatch for tool-intent keywords
findings.description: "Convertir foto en caricatura simpsons"-style queries are dominated (7/10 SERP results) by free instant AI generators. negasva's paid, 48h hand-drawn product page cannot satisfy that intent.
findings.recommendation: Either build a free-preview/instant-generator entry point to capture tool-intent traffic, or deliberately deprioritize these verb-phrase keywords in favor of gift-phrase variants where the site already competes well.

findings.title: Missing gift-occasion framing on style pages
findings.description: Competitors (donutamarillo, mortyme, yellowme) prominently frame products as birthday/anniversary gifts; negasva's style pages don't.
findings.recommendation: Add occasion-based framing and cross-sell prompts on style detail pages.

findings.title: No reviews/ratings visible on style detail pages
findings.description: Authority signal gap identified in gap-scoring (5/15).
findings.recommendation: Surface testimonials or aggregate rating directly on style pages, not just the homepage.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: OnlineStore + Product + AggregateOffer present sitewide
FAQPage JSON-LD covers all 10 Q&A pairs with clean, citable facts
BreadcrumbList present on /precios, /estilos, and style detail pages

findings.title: Competing Product entities on the same page
findings.description: Style pages emit both a generic sitewide Product node and a style-specific Product node, leaving no clear canonical entity for rich results.
findings.recommendation: Remove the generic Product node from style detail pages; keep only the style-specific one.

findings.title: Generic Product/Offer schema on non-product pages
findings.description: Home, /precios index, /estilos index, and /order all carry Product/Offer schema that doesn't belong there.
findings.recommendation: Scope Product schema injection to actual product/style pages only.

findings.title: No Review/AggregateRating schema
findings.description: Visible testimonials with verified-purchase badges aren't marked up, missing rich-result eligibility.
findings.recommendation: Add Review/AggregateRating schema sourced from real, verifiable review data only — fabricated ratings risk a manual action.

findings.title: Identical lowPrice/highPrice across all 4 styles
findings.description: Schema pricing (20/160) is hardcoded identically across styles, suggesting copy-paste rather than per-style data.
findings.recommendation: Derive schema pricing from the same source /precios uses.

findings.title: No WebSite + SearchAction schema
findings.description: Homepage lacks sitelinks-search-box eligibility markup.
findings.recommendation: Add only if a real on-site search endpoint exists.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
what_works: LCP image correctly uses fetchPriority="high", eager loading, and is discoverable in the initial document
CLS passes on home, /galeria, and /estilos/rick-y-morty
TTFB is fast at 304ms

findings.title: LCP fails CWV threshold on all 4 tested pages
findings.description: Measured 2.33s-2.94s ('Needs Improvement') on home, /estilos, /galeria, and /estilos/rick-y-morty.
findings.recommendation: Address render-blocking CSS (9KB, ~178ms) and optimize the hero image further (~41KB available savings).

findings.title: /estilos fails CLS (0.245, 'poor')
findings.description: Root cause confirmed as a dynamically-rendered grid card, not an unsized image or font issue.
findings.recommendation: Reserve layout space for the grid card before its content loads, or render it server-side.

findings.title: Unnecessary legacy JS polyfill
findings.description: ~11KB wasted on an Array.prototype.at polyfill likely from an overly conservative build target.
findings.recommendation: Adjust the JS build target/browserslist to drop unneeded polyfills.

findings.title: Stripe JS loads globally
findings.description: 236KB / 73ms of main-thread time from Stripe's JS loads on pages where checkout isn't yet needed.
findings.recommendation: Lazy-load Stripe JS only on /order or at the point of checkout.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: Pricing and turnaround are stated as clean, repeatable, extractable facts in both prose and schema
Fully SSR'd content is directly crawlable by GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot without JS execution
robots.txt wildcard implicitly allows all major AI crawlers with no blocking rules

findings.title: 9 of 10 FAQ answers invisible outside JSON-LD
findings.description: Only the first FAQ answer renders in visible DOM text; the rest are inside a collapsed accordion using the hidden attribute, removing them from the accessibility tree and plain-text extraction.
findings.recommendation: Use CSS-only collapse (max-height/aria-hidden styling) rather than DOM removal, so all answers remain extractable as plain text.

findings.title: llms.txt missing (404)
findings.description: No llms.txt file exists to summarize the business for AI systems.
findings.recommendation: Add a minimal llms.txt linking to /precios, /faq, /estilos with key facts.

findings.title: No English content despite English search demand
findings.description: Site is Spanish-only; queries like "Rick and Morty portrait" have no dedicated indexable English page.
findings.recommendation: Build at minimum English versions of /precios, /faq, /estilos if international/English-speaking demand is a target market.

findings.title: Weak authority/brand signals for AI citation
findings.description: sameAs schema links only to Instagram/TikTok — platforms with low LLM-citation correlation compared to YouTube/Reddit/Wikipedia.
findings.recommendation: Build YouTube (process videos, before/after reveals) or organic Reddit presence over further generic backlink work.

categories.name: Images
what_works: next/image used with responsive srcset (640w, 750w, etc.) for the hero background
Image delivery infrastructure (Vercel image optimization) is in place and functioning

findings.title: Hero image has unrealized savings
findings.description: Lighthouse flagged ~41KB of available savings on the LCP hero image despite correct fetchPriority/eager-load setup.
findings.recommendation: Re-encode or further compress the hero image; verify the served format (WebP/AVIF) and quality setting.

findings.title: Alt text and image SEO not deeply audited this pass
findings.description: Full alt-text coverage and image-SERP visibility weren't independently verified across all pages.
findings.recommendation: Follow-up audit recommended before treating this category score as final.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Fix canonical tags on /precios, /order, /galeria, /sobre, /contacto, /faq to self-reference
Remove broken en/fr hreflang claims (or commit to building real locales)
Remove competing/duplicate Product schema nodes on style pages
Scope generic Product/Offer schema off non-product pages (home, /order, /precios index)
Add canonical/noindex handling for /order?style=* variants to prevent duplicate-content indexing

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Redirect www.negasva.shop to apex domain
Remove noindex,nofollow from /seguimiento if unintentional
Render all FAQ answers in visible DOM text instead of hidden accordion content
Fix LCP (render-blocking CSS, hero image compression) and /estilos CLS root cause
Add Review/AggregateRating schema using real, verifiable review data
Fix hardcoded identical pricing across the 4 style pages' schema

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Expand all 6 blog posts to substantive length (800-1500 words), starting with the confirmed-thinnest comparison post
Differentiate the 4 style pages beyond name-swapping (unique imagery, FAQ, pricing accuracy)
Add llms.txt and consider an English locale for /precios, /faq, /estilos
Decide deliberately on trademark/IP framing strategy for character-name keywords
Link visible testimonials to a verifiable review platform

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Track Core Web Vitals (LCP/CLS) post-fix via PageSpeed Insights or CrUX once available
Build YouTube/Reddit presence for AI-citation authority signals
Re-run a backlink check once the domain accrues Common Crawl/Moz coverage (currently zero footprint — too new to measure)
Monitor whether "convertir foto en X" tool-intent keywords are worth a dedicated free-preview entry point
